description:
Create a complete Attentional Network Test (ANT) experiment in PsychoPy Builder, following the paradigm established by Fan et al. (2002). The ANT measures three attention networks (alerting, orienting, executive control) by crossing cue and flanker manipulations.

Each trial sequence: variable fixation cross (400-1600ms, uniformly randomized) followed by a cue flash (100ms), then a blank fixation interval (400ms), then the target arrow flanked by congruent, incongruent, or neutral arrows displayed until response or 1700ms timeout. Participants press 'left' and 'right' arrow keys to indicate the direction of the central target arrow.

Create a conditions file ant_conditions.csv with the full factorial design: 4 cue types (none, center, double, spatial) x 3 flanker types (congruent, incongruent, neutral) x 2 target locations (above, below fixation) x 2 target directions (left, right) = 48 rows. Required columns: cue_type, flanker_type, target_location, target_direction, corrAns.

Experiment structure must include: an instructions routine explaining the task; a practice block looping over the conditions with per-trial accuracy feedback; a rest screen after practice; three main experimental blocks each looping over the conditions file with rest screens between blocks; and a summary routine displaying mean reaction time and overall accuracy.

A Code component must handle randomizing the fixation duration each trial, constructing the flanker-target stimulus display from the condition variables, controlling cue visibility based on cue_type, and computing summary statistics for the final screen.

Save as /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ant_experiment.psyexp.
